pr.title: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2569 matched three Ice Mountain functions in src/melee/gr/gricemt.c and cleaned up synth declarations/comments. The PR body claims HSD_Synth_80388DC8 and grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC reached 100.0%, with configure --require-protos and ninja run. The loaded bot report independently lists three new gricemt function matches and a .sdata2 improvement, but does not list HSD_Synth_80388DC8 as a new match.

key_files.role: Main Ice Mountain matching work: added auto_inline pragma, expanded assert macros into __assert forms, introduced grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs helper, adjusted locals/source shape, used PAD_STACK, and matched grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC according to PR body and bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gricemt.c hunks around grIceMt_801F71E8, grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801FA0BC
human_pr_text: Brings grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC to 100%
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists 3 new gricemt matches

key_files.role: Removed obsolete residual-match TODO above HSD_Synth_80388DC8 after claimed match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: removes obsolete residual-match note on HSD_Synth_80388DC8

key_files.role: Tightened HSD_Synth_8038B120 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(void).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_Synth_8038B120 changed to void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void)
human_pr_text: tightens HSD_Synth_8038B120 to existing void(void) definition

what_changed: Added `#pragma auto_inline off` before `grIceMt_801F71E8` in gricemt.c.
Replaced several `HSD_ASSERT` and `HSD_ASSERTMSG` macro calls in Ice Mountain functions with explicit `if` checks calling `__assert` using file/message pointers derived from `grIm_803E4068`.
Reworked `grIceMt_801FA0BC` local variable shape: `mgobj` became `gobj`, `gp2` became `gp`, `f` became `frame`, temporary `id` was removed, and `PAD_STACK(24)` was added.
Removed two wrapper inlines around `grIceMt_801F71E8` and introduced `static inline HSD_JObj** grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground* g)` returning `&g->gv.icemt2.xC8`.
Changed `grIceMt_801FA0BC` to use `grIm_804DB570 * Ground_801C0498()` instead of the literal `-20.0f * Ground_801C0498()` in the shown translation-Y expressions.
Removed an obsolete TODO stating `HSD_Synth_80388DC8` was a 97.2% match.
Updated `HSD_Synth_8038B120` declaration in synth.h from unknown return/params to `void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void)`.

smart_moves.text: The synth header update follows the truthful-header principle: once the body/signature is known, replace `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` in the owning header with the concrete prototype.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: tightens HSD_Synth_8038B120 to existing void(void) definition

smart_moves.text: The Ice Mountain work localized a pointer-to-JObj-array access behind a small typed inline helper returning `&g->gv.icemt2.xC8`, avoiding a broader rewrite while giving the compiler a source shape that matched.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: small source-shape helpers around existing Ice Mountain code

smart_moves.text: The PR body recorded narrow verification commands and a GALE01 100.0% report for the claimed functions, which is useful match evidence for later review.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body proves a signature, update the owning header prototype instead of leaving `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` in place.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: synth.h HSD_Synth_8038B120 changed to void(void)

decomp_lessons.lesson: A very small typed helper can be useful as a source-shape tool when accessing nested GroundVars fields, as shown by `grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground* g)` returning `&g->gv.icemt2.xC8` during the `grIceMt_801FA0BC` match.

naming_conventions.name: grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs
naming_conventions.convention: Helper name combines stage/function address context with the returned role (`jobjs`) rather than inventing a broader semantic name.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline HSD_JObj** grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground* g)

naming_conventions.name: gobj, gp, jobj, frame
naming_conventions.convention: Local names were made role-oriented where roles are clear: game object, ground pointer, JObj, and computed frame/offset value.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_Synth_8038B120
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function name was preserved while only the proven signature was tightened.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped compiler behavior change with `#pragma auto_inline off` before `grIceMt_801F71E8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence does not show a balancing pragma or adjacent-function regression analysis; treat this as a tactic needing curator/reviewer caution under the pragma standard.
The PR body gives match verification, but the loaded evidence does not isolate PAD_STACK as necessary by itself.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expanded assert macros into explicit `if (...) __assert(...)` blocks with file/message pointer expressions to match Ice Mountain functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG replacements in grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801FA0BC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This conflicts with the general cleanup preference for assert macros in loaded standards, so it should be treated as PR-specific matching evidence rather than a global style lesson.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed source shape by removing wrapper inlines around `grIceMt_801F71E8` and adding a typed field helper for the JObj array.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Supported as a local match tactic by the PR body and bot report, not as a general rule.

searchable_terms: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that add `#pragma auto_inline off` near stage helper functions and whether they include balancing pragmas.
Search for `PAD_STACK` in `src/melee/gr` to identify stage matching cases where stack padding remained after verification.
Search for Ice Mountain `__assert((char*) &grIm_803E4068 + 0x690` patterns to see whether explicit assert expansion is localized to gricemt matching.
Search for `UNK_RET` or `UNK_PARAMS` removals in baselib headers paired with `python configure.py --require-protos` verification.
Check whether `grIm_804DB570` is the owned symbol for the `-20.0f` literal in Ice Mountain before deriving any data-ownership lesson.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state MERGED, 3 changed files, diff_bytes 7300
human_pr_text: PR body with claimed matches and verification
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all changed files
text_corpus issue_comment: GALE01 bot report with three gricemt matches and .sdata2 improvement
notes: No human review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent and review feedback are unavailable.

The PR body claims `HSD_Synth_80388DC8` reached 100.0%, but the loaded bot report only lists three new gricemt matches; the synth claim is therefore supported by author verification text but not independently echoed in the bot excerpt.

The diff shows tactics that global standards generally discourage without narrow evidence: `#pragma auto_inline off`, `PAD_STACK`, and explicit `__assert` expansion. The PR body and bot report support final function matching, but not the necessity of each tactic in isolation.
No build logs are loaded beyond the PR body's stated `python configure.py --require-protos`, `ninja`, and GALE01 report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Tighten owning header prototypes when the body proves the signature
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2569 changed `HSD_Synth_8038B120` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.h` from `UNK_RET HSD_Synth_8038B120(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void)` after the PR body described it as the existing `void(void)` definition and listed `python configure.py --require-protos` verification.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Small typed helper for nested GroundVars JObj array access
accepted_candidate_records.text: For `grIceMt_801FA0BC`, PR #2569 replaced wrapper inlines around `grIceMt_801F71E8` with `static inline HSD_JObj** grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground* g) { return &g->gv.icemt2.xC8; }`, then used `jobjs[0..3]` in the matched code. This is evidence for a local Ice Mountain source-shape tactic, not a global helper-naming rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution for explicit assert expansion retained for narrow matches
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2569 matched Ice Mountain functions by replacing `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTMSG` calls with explicit `if` + `__assert` blocks using pointer-derived file/message strings. This should not override the existing macro preference, but it may be worth adding an exception note that such expansions need narrow objdiff evidence and should remain PR/local rather than becoming cleanup style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801FA0BC assert changes
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards prefer macros; this PR provides historical evidence of a narrow match exception but not enough to promote a broad style change.

source_update_candidates.title: Require extra ledger evidence for auto_inline pragmas in matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2569 added `#pragma auto_inline off` before `grIceMt_801F71E8`. The loaded diff does not show balancing or adjacent regression notes. Curator may consider whether standards should explicitly require scoped push/pop or adjacency evidence for `auto_inline` pragmas, similar to existing pragma cautions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added #pragma auto_inline off before grIceMt_801F71E8
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards clarification; PR evidence supports occurrence, not a global best practice.

source_update_candidates.title: Ice Mountain gricemt.c functions matched in PR #2569
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/gr/gricemt.c` contains Ice Mountain functions `grIceMt_801F96E0`, `grIceMt_801F993C`, and `grIceMt_801FA0BC`; PR #2569's bot report lists them as new 100.00% matches in unit `main/melee/gr/gricemt`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful searchable historical path fact, but should be curator-approved before adding to source-owned path facts.

rejection_notes: Do not promote explicit `__assert` expansion as a general cleanup standard; loaded standards prefer `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTMSG` when source-level macros match.
Do not infer reviewer approval details; no review comments are loaded.
Do not treat the `.sdata2` bot improvement as intentional data work beyond the report entry; the PR body scopes the work as function matching.
Do not claim `PAD_STACK(24)` or `#pragma auto_inline off` were individually necessary; evidence only shows they were present in a PR that claimed and reported matches.
